title: acmella buphthalmoides
intro: Acmella buphthalmoides, also known as the parlor plant or toothache plant, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Acmella buphthalmoides lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it a popular remedy for toothaches and other oral health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and prevent infections. Acmella buphthalmoides is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, and its leaves can be chewed fresh to release its active compounds and provide relief from toothaches and other oral health issues.
